speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Romeo, come forth; come forth, thou fearful man.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Affliction is enamored of thy parts,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And thou art wedded to calamity.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Father, what news? What is the Prince’s doom?

speaker: ROMEO
text: What sorrow craves acquaintance at my hand

speaker: ROMEO
text: That I yet know not?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Too familiar

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Is my dear son with such sour company.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I bring thee tidings of the Prince’s doom.

speaker: ROMEO
text: What less than doomsday is the Prince’s doom?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: A gentler judgment vanished from his lips:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Not body’s death, but body’s banishment.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ha, banishment? Be merciful, say “death,”

speaker: ROMEO
text: For exile hath more terror in his look,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Much more than death. Do not say “banishment.”

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Here from Verona art thou banishèd.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Be patient, for the world is broad and wide.

speaker: ROMEO
text: There is no world without Verona walls

speaker: ROMEO
text: But purgatory, torture, hell itself.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Hence “banishèd” is “banished from the world,”

speaker: ROMEO
text: And world’s exile is death. Then “banishèd”

speaker: ROMEO
text: Is death mistermed. Calling death “banishèd,”

speaker: ROMEO
text: Thou cutt’st my head off with a golden ax

speaker: ROMEO
text: And smilest upon the stroke that murders me.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: O deadly sin, O rude unthankfulness!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy fault our law calls death, but the kind prince,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Taking thy part, hath rushed aside the law

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And turned that black word “death” to “banishment.”

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: This is dear mercy, and thou seest it not.

speaker: ROMEO
text: ’Tis torture and not mercy. Heaven is here

speaker: ROMEO
text: Where Juliet lives, and every cat and dog

speaker: ROMEO
text: And little mouse, every unworthy thing,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Live here in heaven and may look on her,

speaker: ROMEO
text: But Romeo may not. More validity,

speaker: ROMEO
text: More honorable state, more courtship lives

speaker: ROMEO
text: In carrion flies than Romeo. They may seize

speaker: ROMEO
text: On the white wonder of dear Juliet’s hand

speaker: ROMEO
text: And steal immortal blessing from her lips,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Who even in pure and vestal modesty

speaker: ROMEO
text: Still blush, as thinking their own kisses sin;

speaker: ROMEO
text: But Romeo may not; he is banishèd.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Flies may do this, but I from this must fly.

speaker: ROMEO
text: They are free men, but I am banishèd.

speaker: ROMEO
text: And sayest thou yet that exile is not death?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Hadst thou no poison mixed, no sharp-ground knife,

speaker: ROMEO
text: No sudden mean of death, though ne’er so mean,

speaker: ROMEO
text: But “banishèd” to kill me? “Banishèd”?

speaker: ROMEO
text: O friar, the damnèd use that word in hell.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Howling attends it. How hast thou the heart,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Being a divine, a ghostly confessor,

speaker: ROMEO
text: A sin absolver, and my friend professed,

speaker: ROMEO
text: To mangle me with that word “banishèd”?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou fond mad man, hear me a little speak.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, thou wilt speak again of banishment.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I’ll give thee armor to keep off that word,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Adversity’s sweet milk, philosophy,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To comfort thee, though thou art banishèd.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Yet “banishèd”? Hang up philosophy.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Unless philosophy can make a Juliet,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Displant a town, reverse a prince’s doom,

speaker: ROMEO
text: It helps not, it prevails not. Talk no more.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: O, then I see that madmen have no ears.

speaker: ROMEO
text: How should they when that wise men have no eyes?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Let me dispute with thee of thy estate.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Thou canst not speak of that thou dost not feel.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Wert thou as young as I, Juliet thy love,

speaker: ROMEO
text: An hour but married, Tybalt murderèd,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Doting like me, and like me banishèd,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Then mightst thou speak, then mightst thou tear thy hair

speaker: ROMEO
text: And fall upon the ground as I do now,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Taking the measure of an unmade grave.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Arise. One knocks. Good Romeo, hide thyself.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Not I, unless the breath of heartsick groans,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Mistlike, enfold me from the search of eyes.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hark, how they knock!—Who’s there?—Romeo, arise.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou wilt be taken.—Stay awhile.—Stand up.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Run to my study.—By and by.—God’s will,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: What simpleness is this?—I come, I come.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Who knocks so hard? Whence come you? What’s your will?

speaker: NURSE
text: Let me come in, and you shall know my errand.

speaker: NURSE
text: I come from Lady Juliet.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Welcome, then.

speaker: NURSE
text: O holy friar, O, tell me, holy friar,

speaker: NURSE
text: Where’s my lady’s lord? Where’s Romeo?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: There on the ground, with his own tears made drunk.

speaker: NURSE
text: O, he is even in my mistress’ case,

speaker: NURSE
text: Just in her case. O woeful sympathy!

speaker: NURSE
text: Piteous predicament! Even so lies she,

speaker: NURSE
text: Blubb’ring and weeping, weeping and blubb’ring.—

speaker: NURSE
text: Stand up, stand up. Stand an you be a man.

speaker: NURSE
text: For Juliet’s sake, for her sake, rise and stand.

speaker: NURSE
text: Why should you fall into so deep an O?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: Ah sir, ah sir, death’s the end of all.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Spakest thou of Juliet? How is it with her?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Doth not she think me an old murderer,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Now I have stained the childhood of our joy

speaker: ROMEO
text: With blood removed but little from her own?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Where is she? And how doth she? And what says

speaker: ROMEO
text: My concealed lady to our canceled love?

speaker: NURSE
text: O, she says nothing, sir, but weeps and weeps,

speaker: NURSE
text: And now falls on her bed, and then starts up,

speaker: NURSE
text: And “Tybalt” calls, and then on Romeo cries,

speaker: NURSE
text: And then down falls again.

speaker: ROMEO
text: As if that name,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Shot from the deadly level of a gun,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Did murder her, as that name’s cursèd hand

speaker: ROMEO
text: Murdered her kinsman.—O, tell me, friar, tell me,

speaker: ROMEO
text: In what vile part of this anatomy

speaker: ROMEO
text: Doth my name lodge? Tell me, that I may sack

speaker: ROMEO
text: The hateful mansion.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hold thy desperate hand!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Art thou a man? Thy form cries out thou art.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy tears are womanish; thy wild acts denote

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The unreasonable fury of a beast.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Unseemly woman in a seeming man,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And ill-beseeming beast in seeming both!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou hast amazed me. By my holy order,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I thought thy disposition better tempered.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hast thou slain Tybalt? Wilt thou slay thyself,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And slay thy lady that in thy life lives,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: By doing damnèd hate upon thyself?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Why railest thou on thy birth, the heaven, and earth,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Since birth and heaven and earth all three do meet

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In thee at once, which thou at once wouldst lose?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Fie, fie, thou shamest thy shape, thy love, thy wit,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Which, like a usurer, abound’st in all

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And usest none in that true use indeed

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Which should bedeck thy shape, thy love, thy wit.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy noble shape is but a form of wax,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Digressing from the valor of a man;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy dear love sworn but hollow perjury,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Killing that love which thou hast vowed to cherish;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy wit, that ornament to shape and love,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Misshapen in the conduct of them both,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Like powder in a skilless soldier’s flask,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Is set afire by thine own ignorance,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And thou dismembered with thine own defense.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: What, rouse thee, man! Thy Juliet is alive,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For whose dear sake thou wast but lately dead:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: There art thou happy. Tybalt would kill thee,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But thou slewest Tybalt: there art thou happy.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The law that threatened death becomes thy friend

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And turns it to exile: there art thou happy.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: A pack of blessings light upon thy back;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Happiness courts thee in her best array;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But, like a misbehaved and sullen wench,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou pouts upon thy fortune and thy love.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Take heed, take heed, for such die miserable.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Go, get thee to thy love, as was decreed.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Ascend her chamber. Hence and comfort her.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But look thou stay not till the watch be set,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For then thou canst not pass to Mantua,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Where thou shalt live till we can find a time

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To blaze your marriage, reconcile your friends,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Beg pardon of the Prince, and call thee back

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: With twenty hundred thousand times more joy

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Than thou went’st forth in lamentation.—

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Go before, nurse. Commend me to thy lady,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And bid her hasten all the house to bed,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Which heavy sorrow makes them apt unto.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Romeo is coming.

speaker: NURSE
text: O Lord, I could have stayed here all the night

speaker: NURSE
text: To hear good counsel. O, what learning is!—

speaker: NURSE
text: My lord, I’ll tell my lady you will come.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Do so, and bid my sweet prepare to chide.

speaker: NURSE
text: Here, sir, a ring she bid me give you, sir.

speaker: NURSE
text: Hie you, make haste, for it grows very late.

speaker: ROMEO
text: How well my comfort vvz revived by this!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Go av, good night—and here stands all your state:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Either be gone before the watch be set

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Or by the break of day disguised from hence.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Sojourn in Mantua. I’ll find out your man,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And he shall signify from time to time

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Every good hap to you that chances here.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Give me thy hand. ’Tis late. Farewell. Good night.

speaker: ROMEO
text: But that a joy past joy calls out on me,

speaker: ROMEO
text: It were a grief so brief to part with thee.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Farewell.
